cards.title: Pet Care Teaches Family Nurturing Skills
cards.body: A family's experience raising a rabbit revealed unexpected lessons about patience and care that paralleled raising children. Caring for creatures you can't reason with builds essential parenting skills.

cards.title: Parenting Teens Through A-Levels
cards.body: Supporting a teenager through A-levels can feel helpless for parents, especially when tackling complex subjects like chemistry. The role of parental support during exam stress is often overlooked.

cards.title: Helping Children Process Parent Loss
cards.body: A widowed father found healing by reading his late wife's favorite books with their son, honoring shared memories. This ritual helped him move past guilt and reconnect with his son.

cards.title: Childcare Costs Put Families at Risk
cards.body: Rising childcare costs are making it harder for families to afford raising children in Britain. Government intervention is needed to prevent profiteers from exploiting working parents.

cards.title: Family Estrangement and Child Wellbeing
cards.body: Family estrangement isn't a simple cut-off but exists on a spectrum of reduced contact. Research shows wellbeing effects are mixed, and support is crucial whether reconciliation happens or not.

cards.title: Emotional Intelligence Starts With Naming Feelings
cards.body: Teaching children to name their emotions gives them agency over their feelings and builds emotional intelligence. This foundational skill supports long-term mental health and resilience.

cards.title: Childhood Abuse Normalizes Harmful Relationships
cards.body: Survivors of childhood abuse often blame themselves for 'missing signs' of harmful behavior. When abuse was normalized early, toxic relationships can feel familiar and safe.

cards.title: Births Fall to 50-Year Low in UK
cards.body: Live births in England and Wales hit their lowest level since 1977, with the average age of first-time mothers continuing to rise. Economic uncertainty and concerns about child-rearing are major factors.

cards.title: Parent Burnout and Moral Injury
cards.body: A parent 'losing control' in a meeting often reflects the slow accumulation of being overruled in systems designed to dismiss them. Understanding this context reframes parental stress as a systemic issue.
